acceptance_rule_shape: AcceptDInputCandidateB0 requires discharge of every listed field obligation

obligations.obligation: DetectorCandidateB0 is a bounded opaque identifier within the closed candidate record
SupportCandidateB0 is finite and contained in ScaledEnergyObservableDomainB

T_total_candidate includes field and detector contributions; partial_mu T_total_candidate^{mu nu} = 0 and partial_mu tau_nu = 0 throughout SupportCandidateB0, implying partial_mu J_E_candidate^mu = 0 for J_E_candidate^mu := T_total_candidate^{mu nu} * tau_nu
M_D > 0 and DetectorOutputCandidateB is a bounded declared codomain
detector_count is declared dimensionless and is compatible with DetectorOutputCandidateB
R_D_candidate is total on the admitted bounded input domain and returns DetectorOutputCandidateB
independent bounded values for k_D and b_D are supplied and justify readout_D = k_D * y_D + b_D
for every admitted input, abs(R_D_candidate(admitted_input)) <= M_D
DistinctFromConstantRescaling_D is witnessed by one admitted input without assuming the desired inequality

blocked_promotions: acceptance obligations -> detector-response input contract inhabited
non_claims: does not discharge any acceptance obligation
does not inhabit the detector-response input contract
does not prove finite detector support
does not prove local current conservation
does not calibrate k_D or b_D
does not prove the finite-output rule
next_weakest_point: Refine the finite-support obligation into an executable candidate verifier without discharging it.
